channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Moderate 6.1 Magnitude Earthquake Hits Hokkaido Region Without Immediate Damage
item.description: A preliminary magnitude 6.1 earthquake struck northern Japan in the early hours of April 27, 2026, as reported by the United States Geological Survey (USGS). The tremor occurred in Hokkaido, one of Ja...

item.title: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe Becomes First Man to Run a Sub-Two-Hour Marathon in Official Race
item.description: Kenya's Sabastian Sawe has made history by becoming the first man to officially run a marathon in under two hours, clocking 1:59:30 at the 2026 London Marathon. Sawe, unbeaten in all marathons since h...

item.title: Sundowns Drop Points Again as Pirates Fail to Extend Lead
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns missed another opportunity to take top spot in the Betway Premiership after they were held to a 0-0 draw by Richards Bay FC in a match at Umhlatuze Sports Complex. This followed a di...

item.title: Master Hanz Cua's Feng Shui Forecast for April 27, 2026
item.description: On April 27, 2026, Master Hanz Cua offers his feng shui predictions, focusing on how various elements of the Chinese metaphysical system can influence personal and professional aspects of life. Known ...

item.title: Garissa Woman in Custody Over Potentially Inciting Social Media Videos
item.description: A woman from Garissa, Bare Sahara Ahmed, has been arrested following the spread of a viral video that authorities say contains statements likely to undermine national cohesion. The video shows Sahara ...

item.title: NRS Denies Any New Vehicle Tax, Warns Public Against False Info
item.description: The Nigeria Revenue Service (NRS) don clarify say no new vehicle tax don come up, despite wahala wey dey go round for social media. According to the NRS, some viral message dey claim say from July 1, ...

item.title: Atiku Urges Government Not to Suspend Political Activities in Northern Nigeria Due to Insecurity
item.description: Atiku Abubakar, the former Vice President of Nigeria, has expressed concern over the potential suspension of political activities in certain Northern states due to the escalating insecurity ahead of t...

item.title: Suspect in D.C. Correspondents’ Dinner shooting traveled from California and allegedly planned attack in advance
item.description: Authorities say Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old Caltech graduate and tutor from Torrance, California, traveled across the country by train before carrying out a shooting near the White House Correspon...

item.title: Mali Defence Minister Dies in Major Assault by al Qaeda-Linked Fighters
item.description: The Defence Minister of Mali, Sadio Camara, was tragically killed in an attack by al Qaeda-linked group JNIM at his home on the Kati military base near Bamako on Saturday. The violence was part of a w...

item.title: Pakistan Opens Six Land Transit Routes to Iran Amid Port Blockade
item.description: In response to the ongoing blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and Iranian ports, Pakistan has officially notified six land routes for transporting goods to Iran. The Ministry of Commerce issued the 'Tra...

item.title: Trump and administration officials targeted in Washington shooting attempt
item.description: On April 26, 2026, a shooting incident occurred during the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner in Washington, where President Donald Trump and several officials from his administration were...

item.title: Pokot Family Left Stranded After Lawyer Fails to Deliver Accident Compensation
item.description: A family from Pokot county is facing financial and emotional strain after their lawyer allegedly vanished with KSh 2.8 million meant as compensation for a fatal road accident in Kikuyu, Kiambu county ...

item.title: Sundowns Draw Against Richards Bay, Miss Opportunity to Leapfrog Pirates
item.description: Mamelodi Sundowns failed to capitalise on Orlando Pirates’ dropped points in the Soweto Derby, settling for a 0-0 draw against Richards Bay FC at the Umhlatuze Sports Complex. This result means that S...

item.title: Pakistan Airports Authority introduces electric cart service for passengers
item.description: The Pakistan Airports Authority (PAA) is all set to launch an electric cart service at major airports, starting with Islamabad, Karachi, Lahore, and Multan. This move is aimed at easing passenger move...

item.title: Sabastian Sawe Sets New Marathon World Record, Top 10 Times Revealed
item.description: Kenyan runner Sabastian Sawe has made history at the London Marathon, smashing the world record and electrifying the running world. The event saw incredible performances across the board, prompting a ...

item.title: Pirates and Chiefs share points in intense Soweto derby amid safety concerns
item.description: Orlando Pirates and Kaizer Chiefs played out a hard-fought 1-1 draw in a lively Betway Premiership Soweto derby at a packed FNB Stadium on Sunday. The match delivered plenty of action, with both sides...

item.title: Chaos Erupts at White House Correspondents' Dinner After Gunman Breach
item.description: During the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner, which was set to feature President Donald Trump's speech, chaos erupted when an attempted gunman breach caused panic among the attending jour...

item.title: Government plans tougher measures to tackle shoplifting and protect retail workers
item.description: Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer is set to announce a series of measures aimed at addressing rising concerns over shoplifting and the safety of retail workers across the UK. Speaking publicly, he is ex...

item.title: Exploring the Controversial Michael Jackson Biopic and Its Timely Release
item.description: The new Michael Jackson biopic is generating buzz, but its journey to the screen has been far from smooth. Owen Myers, deputy arts editor for Guardian US, and Lanre Bakare, arts and culture correspond...

item.title: NYSC corps member dies in Abuja crossfire during armed robbery response
item.description: A serving National Youth Service Corps member, Abdulsamad Jamiu, has died after being caught in the crossfire during a security operation in Abuja. According to the Nigerian Army, the incident happene...
